creator: shon@misproductions.com
text:
Created attachment 163098
Current Snapshot Release page saved as HTML to show the above.

Under the FreeBSD Snapshots Page
url: https://www.freebsd.org/snapshots/

It says:
"Currently the snapshots of 11-CURRENT, 10-STABLE, and 10-STABLE, are available in directories whose URLs have the format ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/snapshots/<target>/<target_arch>/ where <target> and <target_arch> represent the architecture for which the snapshot was built. "

There are two 10-STABLE listed with the same target link which seems a bit redundant. Shouldn't one of those be 10-RELEASE or even omitted instead?
